title: wyred contract v0 — standalone allocation artifact (.alloc.json)
description: Descriptive schema for the standalone allocation artifact, extracted from its producer wyred/resolve.py (`ResolveResult.alloc`, serialized by `alloc_json_str`) — there is NO prose for this kind (F1: the wrapper is a golden+engine artifact, not Part C). F1 distinction: this is a WRAPPER around the Part-C allocation record, not the bare record — it has EXACTLY the eight keys allocation/bindings/bound_parts/connector_pinout/series/solver_version/stamp/wiring, all always emitted. `allocation` is the shared Part-C record; `bindings` maps a role-id to its single bound refdes (emitted only for roles with exactly one part, so the map is a subset of bound_parts' keys); `bound_parts` maps every role-id to its realized part rows; `wiring` is the landed-allocation view (F9 dotted nodes); `connector_pinout` is the external-interface ICD rows. additionalProperties:false everywhere (closed shape throughout).

series.description: document series identity (board-spin semantics), copied from the l1 doc

bindings.description: map <role-id> -> <refdes> for roles bound to EXACTLY ONE part (resolve.py emits a binding only when len(parts)==1); a subset of bound_parts' keys. May be {} when every role binds multiple parts.

bound_parts.description: map <role-id> -> array of the role's realized part rows (refdes/part/chosen_by).

wiring.description: landed-allocation view: one row per realized allocation entry, with its dotted node (F9) and net.

connector_pinout.description: external-interface ICD rows; [] when the design declares no connectors. locked_by is set once an external-interface (connector_pinout-covering) lock group fires.
